description: Track the initial build of imux as an interactive multiplexer, including core process lifecycle control, live terminal UI, dynamic process management, and process inspection capabilities.

Implement output multiplexing with stream-aware logging
description: Build output multiplexing for managed processes with optional logging to file. Preserve stream identity so stdout and stderr are differentiated in UI and log output, instead of merged text streams.

Build imux interactive multiplexer MVP
description: Track the initial build of imux as an interactive multiplexer, including core process lifecycle control, live terminal UI, dynamic process management, and process inspection capabilities.

Implement output multiplexing with stream-aware logging
description: Build output multiplexing for managed processes with optional logging to file. Use a single merged output stream/log while preserving source identity so stdout and stderr are explicitly differentiated on every emitted line/event.
acceptance: Marker format is simple and ergonomic (e.g., o/e prefix), finalized during implementation

description.old: Build output multiplexing for managed processes with optional logging to file. Preserve stream identity so stdout and stderr are differentiated in UI and log output, instead of merged text streams.
description.new: Build output multiplexing for managed processes with optional logging to file. Use a single merged output stream/log while preserving source identity so stdout and stderr are explicitly differentiated on every emitted line/event.

acceptance.old: Log format supports reliable replay/parsing without losing ordering context
acceptance.new: Marker format is simple and ergonomic (e.g., o/e prefix), finalized during implementation

title: Define imux architecture and process model
Define imux architecture and process model
description: Define core architecture for imux: supervisor model, process abstraction, event bus, state store, CLI entrypoint design, and boundaries between backend process control and TUI rendering. The CLI must use spf13/cobra for argument parsing and command structure, and should provide ergonomic single-letter top-level commands/aliases for tmux-heavy workflows.

Document and enforce issue close transaction helper
description: Track the newly added close-workflow guardrails in imux: strict closing order in CLAUDE.md, a just close-checklist helper, and an enforced scripts/close-issue.sh transaction command that closes, commits, merges, and pushes in one flow. Also require explicit --commit-message for deterministic commit intent.
acceptance: Issue documents the workflow guardrails and can be closed via the enforced close helper using an explicit commit message.

acceptance.new: Issue documents the workflow guardrails and can be closed via the enforced close helper using an explicit commit message.

Harden close-issue argument handling for quoted text
description: Fix argument forwarding so just close-issue reliably supports multi-word --reason and --commit-message values without breaking parsing. Current recipe tokenizes values unexpectedly and only works with single-token values. Update invocation shape and docs/examples accordingly.
acceptance: Running close helper supports quoted multi-word reason and commit message values without argument-splitting failures.

description.new: Fix argument forwarding so just close-issue reliably supports multi-word --reason and --commit-message values without breaking parsing. Current recipe tokenizes values unexpectedly and only works with single-token values. Update invocation shape and docs/examples accordingly.

title: Harden close-issue argument handling for quoted text
Harden close-issue argument handling for quoted text
description: Fix argument forwarding so just close-issue reliably supports multi-word --reason and --commit-message values without breaking parsing. Current recipe tokenizes values unexpectedly and only works with single-token values. Update invocation shape and docs/examples accordingly.
acceptance: Running close helper supports quoted multi-word reason and commit message values without argument-splitting failures.
notes: Hardened close helper invocation by replacing variadic just passthrough with explicit positional recipe arguments. This preserves quoted multi-word reason/commit-message values. Validation:  reached status validation (no argument splitting).

Build alt-screen TUI shell and layout
description: Implement Bubble Tea alt-screen application shell with panes for process list, selected process output, and inspector panel placeholders.
acceptance: UI state is driven by central model, not ad-hoc globals
notes: Implemented Bubble Tea alt-screen shell with central  state in internal/tui/model.go. Added three-pane layout placeholders (process list, output, inspector), keyboard navigation (tab/h/l focus, j/k selection, q quit), and removed prior tui scaffold by wiring CLI command to tui.Run(). Validation: go test ./... passes.

title: Build alt-screen TUI shell and layout
Build alt-screen TUI shell and layout
description: Bubble Tea alt-screen TUI: one full-width merged stdout/stderr log view (placeholder stream until imux-38oz). Process list, inspector, and help are on-demand centered overlays; log lines stay visible and keep refreshing under overlays (composite render + tea.Tick). Central model in internal/tui; no globals.
acceptance: UI state is driven by central model, not ad-hoc globals
notes: Shipped: internal/tui (model.go, overlay.go) + cli tui -> tui.Run(). Layout: single log surface; p/i/? open overlays; Esc closes (help returns prior overlay). Log never replaced wholesale—compositeLogWithModal splices modal rows with charmbracelet/x/ansi. Placeholder lines include tick counter; tea.Tick every 400ms. Keys: q quit; , . prev/next process; j/k in process list; Enter confirms. justfile run recipe documents not using 'just run -- <subcmd>' (literal -- breaks Cobra). Validation: go test ./... passes.

Implement process lifecycle controls
description: Implement per-process controls: pause, continue, kill, and restart, with consistent state transitions and user-visible outcomes.
acceptance: Errors are surfaced in UI and logs with actionable messages

title: Implement process lifecycle controls
Implement process lifecycle controls
description: Implement per-process controls: pause, continue, kill, and restart, with consistent state transitions and user-visible outcomes.
acceptance: Errors are surfaced in UI and logs with actionable messages
notes: Implemented ExecSupervisor (Register/Start/Stop/Kill/Pause/Continue/Restart/List) with in-memory ChanEventBus and MapStateStore; unix process-group signals (SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGSTOP/SIGCONT) and Windows stop/kill path. Extended core states/events for paused/resumed and operator EventProcessError. Wired imux run to real multirun-style execution with stderr event stream and fail-fast. TUI: demo processes + lifecycle keys s/t/k/z/v/y, inspector shows store state, event bus lines merged into log; quit stops children. SetStopGrace honors run --grace. Tests: lifecycle table + exec supervisor true-exit + pause/continue on unix. Validation: go test ./...

notes.new: Implemented ExecSupervisor (Register/Start/Stop/Kill/Pause/Continue/Restart/List) with in-memory ChanEventBus and MapStateStore; unix process-group signals (SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGSTOP/SIGCONT) and Windows stop/kill path. Extended core states/events for paused/resumed and operator EventProcessError. Wired imux run to real multirun-style execution with stderr event stream and fail-fast. TUI: demo processes + lifecycle keys s/t/k/z/v/y, inspector shows store state, event bus lines merged into log; quit stops children. SetStopGrace honors run --grace. Tests: lifecycle table + exec supervisor true-exit + pause/continue on unix. Validation: go test ./...

close_reason: Supervisor pipes stdout/stderr into EventProcessOutput with o/e stream tags; imux run prints merged lines to stdout and honors --tee; TUI shows same format.

title: Support dynamic process creation from UI
TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
description:
MVP backlog covered stream tagging (imux-38oz) but not the main usability win from gastrolog multirun: a single merged view where every line is immediately attributable and scannable via color + bracketed process name.

Reference implementation: gastrolog backend/cmd/multirun/main.go — fixed ANSI palette cycled by child index, lineWriter mutex so concurrent scanners cannot interleave partial lines, bufio.Scanner line boundaries, writeTo(prefix,color,line) formatting, optional tee without ANSI.

Current imux: EventProcessOutput is appended as plain text in internal/tui/model.go (drainEvents / appendLogLine); composeLines still pads with placeholder stream lines when the buffer is short. There is no per-process lipgloss/ANSI styling in the merged surface, so dense multi-process output is harder to read than multirun.

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with imux run output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees.
notes: External ref: ~/Code/gastrolog/backend/cmd/multirun/main.go (lineWriter, colors, scanLines, startChild pipe wiring).

title: TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
description:
MVP backlog covered stream tagging (imux-38oz) but not the main usability win from gastrolog multirun: a single merged view where every line is immediately attributable and scannable via color + bracketed process name.

Reference implementation: gastrolog backend/cmd/multirun/main.go — fixed ANSI palette cycled by child index, lineWriter mutex so concurrent scanners cannot interleave partial lines, bufio.Scanner line boundaries, writeTo(prefix,color,line) formatting, optional tee without ANSI.

Current imux: EventProcessOutput is appended as plain text in internal/tui/model.go (drainEvents / appendLogLine); composeLines still pads with placeholder stream lines when the buffer is short. There is no per-process lipgloss/ANSI styling in the merged surface, so dense multi-process output is harder to read than multirun.

## Storage and memory

- Do not hold the full merged log in RAM. Persist session lines to a file-backed store.
- Use an unlinked temp file (open temp, then unlink path while keeping the fd) so the log does not clutter the filesystem and is reclaimed when the process exits.
- Add a `--tee` flag (TUI entrypoint, analogous to `imux run --tee`) for an optional persisted path that receives plain, non-ANSI merged lines.

## TUI log UX

- Togglable visibility for stdout vs stderr (and clear UI affordance for current state).
- Togglable timestamps on displayed lines.
- Scroll the log view: Page Up / Page Down and mouse scroll wheel (enable appropriate Bubble Tea mouse mode).
- Filter the log: support both glob and regex (document how the user selects mode, e.g. prefix `glob:` / `re:` or separate flags).

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with `imux run` output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees. Log content is not kept in an unbounded in-memory slice: primary retention is the unlinked temp backing file. `--tee` writes plain non-ANSI lines to the given path. TUI supports toggling stdout/stderr and timestamps, scrolling via PgUp/PgDn and wheel, and filtering (glob and regex).
notes: External ref: ~/Code/gastrolog/backend/cmd/multirun/main.go (lineWriter, colors, scanLines, startChild pipe wiring).

title: TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
description:
MVP backlog covered stream tagging (imux-38oz) but not the main usability win from gastrolog multirun: a single merged view where every line is immediately attributable and scannable via color + bracketed process name.

Reference implementation: gastrolog backend/cmd/multirun/main.go — fixed ANSI palette cycled by child index, lineWriter mutex so concurrent scanners cannot interleave partial lines, bufio.Scanner line boundaries, writeTo(prefix,color,line) formatting, optional tee without ANSI.

Current imux: EventProcessOutput is appended as plain text in internal/tui/model.go (drainEvents / appendLogLine); composeLines still pads with placeholder stream lines when the buffer is short. There is no per-process lipgloss/ANSI styling in the merged surface, so dense multi-process output is harder to read than multirun.

## Unified log (single multiplexed stream)

The merged log must stay one chronological interleaving of all processes (multiplex/gastrolog multirun semantics) for use cases like multi-node clusters: one mental model, one tail to watch.

Do **not** swap or replace the log surface when the dock selection changes. Today placeholder filler in composeLines/placeholderStreamLines keys off the focused process name, which makes the log appear to switch with selection—that is wrong and should be removed or replaced with neutral filler. Changing selection may update the dock/inspector only; the log remains the same unified stream (subject only to user filters/toggles/scroll), not a per-process log view.

## Storage and memory

- Do not hold the full merged log in RAM. Persist session lines to a file-backed store.
- Use an unlinked temp file (open temp, then unlink path while keeping the fd) so the log does not clutter the filesystem and is reclaimed when the process exits.
- Add a `--tee` flag (TUI entrypoint, analogous to `imux run --tee`) for an optional persisted path that receives plain, non-ANSI merged lines.

## TUI log UX

- Togglable visibility for stdout vs stderr (and clear UI affordance for current state).
- Togglable timestamps on displayed lines.
- Scroll the log view: Page Up / Page Down and mouse scroll wheel (enable appropriate Bubble Tea mouse mode).
- Filter the log: support both glob and regex (document how the user selects mode, e.g. prefix `glob:` / `re:` or separate flags).

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with `imux run` output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees. Log content is not kept in an unbounded in-memory slice: primary retention is the unlinked temp backing file. `--tee` writes plain non-ANSI lines to the given path. TUI supports toggling stdout/stderr and timestamps, scrolling via PgUp/PgDn and wheel, and filtering (glob and regex). The log is a single unified multiplexed timeline: changing dock focus does not swap or re-scope the log to the selected process only.
notes: External ref: ~/Code/gastrolog/backend/cmd/multirun/main.go (lineWriter, colors, scanLines, startChild pipe wiring).

Removed selection-dependent placeholder stream; neutral empty state. Help/footer updated. Branch task/imux-1zcu-unified-log. go test ./... go vet ./...
